title: cassia fasciculata var. littoralis
intro: Cassia fasciculata var. littoralis, also known as the Coastal Senna or Yellow Senna, is a plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the coastal regions of the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cassia fasciculata var. littoralis include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and pods can be used to make herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures.
